name: Singapore Curry Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. All-purpose flour
2. Egg
3. Milk - 2/3 cup
4. Cold water - 2/3 cup
5. Corn oil - 5 tablespoons
6. Onion, chopped
7. Lean ground beef - 8 ounces
8. Carrots, grated
9. Parsnips, grated
10. Curry powder - 2 teaspoons
11. Tomato paste - 1 tablespoon
12. Cornstarch - 2 teaspoons
13. Beef stock - 2/3 cup
14. Egg, beaten
15. Vegetable oil for frying
1. Sift the flour into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add the egg.
2. Gradually stir in milk and beat until smooth. Stir in cold water and beat well.
3. Pour batter into a pitcher.
4. Heat a little oil in a small skillet and pour off excess. Pour a little batter into the skillet, swirling to spread evenly over the bottom to make a thin coating.
5. Fry until the underside is golden; turn out of the pan (do not cook the other side). Repeat with remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan each time, to make 8 pancakes.
6. Heat corn oil in a saucepan. Add onion, ground beef, carrots, parsnips, and curry powder. Cook gently for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
7. Add tomato paste and mix well. Blend cornstarch with a little stock.
8. Add the remaining stock to the meat mixture and bring to a boil. Add cornstarch mixture and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
9. Simmer the mixture for 10 minutes. Place pancakes on a flat surface, cooked sides up.
10. Spread filling in two horizontal lines across the center, within 1-1/2 inches of the side edges.
11. Fold the side edges over the filling, then fold the remaining top and bottom edges to cover it.
12. Brush with beaten egg and fold pancakes in half. Chill for 1 hour.
13. Heat oil in a deep fat fryer or saucepan to 350°F (175°C). Fry folded pancakes, four at a time, for 2-3 minutes or until golden brown and heated through. Drain on paper towels.
14. Garnish with carrot strips if desired, and serve hot.
